title: Installing podman
body:
Add documentation on how to install a packaged version of podman

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: I think there's a Gentoo overlay to automate the build process. And Arch has Podman in the AUR (named libpod, strangely - https://aur.archlinux.org/packages/libpod/)

@giuseppe @vrothberg @mheon @baude @TomSweeneyRedHat @cevich @edsantiago PTAL
Lets do another review and get this merged.  We can always fix these going forward.  Need this page for podman.1.0 announcement.

@giuseppe @vrothberg @mheon @baude @TomSweeneyRedHat @cevich @edsantiago PTAL
Lets do another review and get this merged.  We can always fix these going forward.  Need this page for podman.1.0 announcement.
Two nits, but the way the Markdown displays is identical with or without the newlines, so no need to make the changes unless we need to fix something else

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@rhatdan FWIW / FYI: You can totally bypass CI  by sticking ``[skip ci]`` in the PR title.  Not a big deal either way, and I don't care about the VMs.  Rather it saves (expensive) engineer-time waiting for mostly useless results (besides maybe some minor commit-level verification) :smiley:
